insight: When facing Vai Mauronax's freeze threat, the correct response is to click your primary absorber (e.g., Arka) every turn rather than holding it to absorb on a secondary unit. Holding creates a fork where Vai can freeze the Wall instead, forcing you to overdefend even more. Just click the absorber and accept not getting the secondary absorb.
author: amalloy
context: Discussion of how to respond to Vai Mauronax freeze threat when both Arka and Centurion are in the set.

insight: Vai Mauronax is a relatively weak double-Animus unit. A second Animus is typically only justified for units like Ampere or Lucina, not for Vai, even when the set appears to make Vai look attractive. The freeze and fork threat does not compensate for the high cost (2nd Animus + 2nd Forge investment) relative to the tempo gained.
author: mrguy888
context: Analysis of a set with Vai Mauronax, Centurion, and Arka where a second Animus was considered.

insight: A second absorber is only worth buying against Vai Mauronax's freeze if that absorber is also useful as pure soak (e.g., Wall). Otherwise, the threat of the 1 extra Vai freeze and forking does not justify the investment.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of whether to build a second absorber to counter Vai Mauronax's freeze denial.

insight: Thunderhead (11 HP, 3 attack) is good against rush builds that lack the resources to burst 11 damage efficiently, but is bad when the opponent can easily burst 11 attack (e.g., Grenade Mech, Cluster Bolt) or deny its absorb (e.g., Thermite Core, Scorchilla, Lancetooth).
author: willflame
context: Discussion of when Thunderhead is a good or bad purchase.

insight: Against a Tatsu Nullifier player, the best defense is offense. Rushing the Tatsu player as fast as possible (e.g., with Tesla Coil) forces them to stop buying Tatsus, limiting their threat to 1 or 2 units. Tesla Coil is specifically strong at this because it pressures immediately.
author: jamberine
context: Discussion of how to counter a Tatsu Nullifier strategy, with the opening line CCB/tesla+E/tesla+E/FFSEE proposed.

insight: In sets with Antima Comet and Galvani Drone, Antima Comet functions as the best attacker and should be rushed early. Starting with Conduit and then purchasing Blastforge-Animus on the same turn is efficient because green energy can be stored while blue and red require spending gold immediately.
author: willflame
context: Replay review of a Galvantima game where the player lost due to having second Antima Comet.

insight: Having Antima Comet one or two turns behind your opponent is very punishing because the opponent's Antima kills the Engineers that would have fueled your own Antima. If forced into second Antima, the recommended response is to buy Walls and defensive structures to absorb the damage, build a larger economy, and then counter with Antima later, potentially transitioning into Cauterizers for additional Engineers.
author: willflame
context: Post-game analysis of a Galvantima game where the player lost to Antima Comet.

insight: Venge Cannon should be built as late as possible, just in time to stop incoming damage, while spending the intervening turns building drones and Conduits. Rushing Venge Cannon too early sacrifices economy and makes the player vulnerable to units like Thunderhead.
author: amalloy
context: Analysis of a Breachproof set featuring Venge Cannon, Fabricator, and Iso Kronus.

insight: In Breachproof sets with Venge Cannon, becoming breachproof is achieved by having no squishy units (like drones) to breach, not simply by building a Fabricator. Allowing drones to be breached when it could be avoided is a significant mistake.
author: amalloy
context: Analysis of a Breachproof set; player was letting drones get breached despite having Fabricator.

insight: Fibroid is weak in Breachproof sets because it only provides 2 soak for 3GR cost and generates no attack threat, making it ineffective at applying the pressure needed to matter in a BP game.
author: xyotsuba
context: Discussion of which units are playable in a Venge Cannon Breachproof set.

insight: Tesla Coil effectively attacks for a net of 2 damage per turn (3 to opponent, 1 to self via Engineer cost), alongside receiving the Engineers as byproduct units. It is strong as a rush tool against opponents who commit to Animus too quickly, but is not inherently overpowered given its total cost of 11GGB.
author: amalloy
context: Discussion of whether Tesla Coil is overpowered after a player lost to Tesla Coil rushes.

insight: In sets with Doomed Mech, a third Engineer may be justified because Doomed Mech benefits from the additional energy production. This is in contrast to units like Grenade Mech or Bombarder where a third Engineer is less often warranted.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of whether to buy 2 or 3 Engineers when Doomed Mech or Bombarder are in the set.

insight: Buying attack as P2 (e.g., Animus + Tarsier on turn 1) gives your opponent the opportunity to get ahead on economy, which is why going Animus-Tarsier-Tarsier as P2 is generally considered a mistake in most sets.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of the econ trade-off when P2 buys early attack.

insight: The transition window from 'drones are good to buy' to 'drones are bad to buy' is typically very short, sometimes zero turns. Players should not expect a long comfortable period of drone buying before switching to units.
author: amalloy
context: Brief discussion about when to stop buying drones and start buying units.

insight: When a unit with an 'Ebb' ability is in your hand, you should always click it at the start of your turn and only unclick it if your resulting turn ends up buying a Drone. This way you use normal drone-buying judgment without needing any special Ebb knowledge.
author: mrguy888
context: Replay review where the player failed to click their Ebb units, losing significant income.

insight: Steelsplitter is considered one of the weakest units in the game. Buying a Steelsplitter in the opening (e.g., turn 3) instead of spending gold on economy or better units is a significant mistake because the single point of threat it generates barely forces a defensive response, and the 6-gold investment is rarely recouped.
author: consensus
context: Replay analysis where buying a Steelsplitter on turn 3 was identified as a key mistake.

insight: Forcing an opponent to build a Wall by generating 1 attack via a 6-gold Steelsplitter, when they only pay 5 gold for the Wall half a turn later, is not a good trade, especially when gold was floated to do so and a significantly worse unit was purchased.
author: jamberine
context: Critique of a Steelsplitter purchase intended to slow the opponent by forcing a Wall.

insight: In sets with Scorchilla, low-econ Scorchilla sets require buying Scorchilla early as P1 (e.g., not starting with a Conduit). Buying Conduit first as P1 in a low-econ Scorchilla set is considered incorrect.
author: mrguy888
context: Replay analysis of a Scorchilla set where P1 opened with a Conduit instead of Scorchilla.

insight: More random units in the set generally reduces player-favouring (the advantage conferred by going first or second) because more strategies become available, giving both players more options to respond.
author: willflame
context: Discussion of whether removing base+5 queue setting (fewer units) increases player-favouring.

insight: In a Deadeye Operative mirror match, DD DD DBB is the correct P2 opening. The DA opening appears to lose to the DD DDE DDC line.
author: mrguy888
context: Brief discussion of correct opening theory in a Deadeye Operative mirror.

insight: In sets with Galvani Drone, if you would otherwise float gold, buying an Engineer followed by a Galvani Drone on the next turn (using the Engineer's energy) is better than floating, though still inferior to buying a Conduit if green is available.
author: jamberine
context: Opening theory discussion where a player was floating gold instead of using Engineer + Galvani Drone.

insight: When holding a unit with a freeze or absorb-denial ability (like Vai Mauronax) is in play, granularity of your defensive units matters: not clicking a Rhino on a turn when you don't need to spend it can give the opponent a free hold on that Rhino if you later ruin your granularity.
author: amalloy
context: Replay analysis noting a granularity mistake with Rhino that gifted the opponent a free hold.

insight: Against a Bloodrager double-buy threat, buying a Rhino is generally better than buying a Wall because Rhino provides more effective soak against the double Bloodrager attack pattern.
author: siepu
context: Brief discussion about optimal defense against double Bloodrager threat.

insight: Cauterizer combined with Drake and Doomed Mech is considered very strong: Cauterizer bypasses the absorb that both Drake and Doomed Mech provide, making the combination highly efficient offensively.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of unit synergies in a set with Drake, Doomed Mech, Cauterizer, and Tarsier.

insight: In sets with Tia Threnody and Protoplasm, double Thorium Dynamo into Breachproof loses even when the Tia player responds with their own Tia immediately, because the Breachproof player cannot threaten Tia until it is too late to matter.
author: jamberine
context: Jamberine analyzing how to beat a Tia Threnody + Protoplasm strategy in a specific set.

insight: In Prismata community usage, 'casual bots' refers specifically to bots played via the 'casual match' (Quick Play) queue, as distinct from bots in the computer section. The term is used to help players locate which game section contains the bot, not just to describe difficulty.
author: mrguy888
context: Debate about whether calling bots 'casual bots' is meaningful, resolved by clarifying it indicates the queue section.

insight: BottyMcBotFace is considered roughly equivalent to Master Bot in overall strength, not a 'casual bot' in the pejorative sense. It reportedly has a larger opening book than Master Bot, but the rest of its decision-making is considered the same as Master Bot.
author: shadourow
context: Discussion about whether BottyMcBotFace deserves to be called a 'casual bot' and comparison of bot strengths.
